title: How Kundalini Awakening Destroys Relationships (And Sometimes That's Right)

body:
How Kundalini Awakening Destroys Relationships (And Sometimes That's Right)

You're probably reading this at 3 AM, right? Staring at the ceiling, feeling like your world's been ripped apart. Maybe your partner just walked out, or you're the one who can't stand to be in the same room with them anymore. You're wondering what the hell is happening, why everything feels so wrong, and if you're losing your mind. I get it. I've been there. I've seen it. Kundalini did a number on my relationships, and it's doing one on yours too, isn't it?

Here's the thing: when that energy hits, it's not playing nice. It's not asking for permission. It's a furnace, and it burns through everything that isn't real, everything that isn't aligned with who you truly are. And a lot of times, that includes your relationships. The ones you thought were solid, the ones you built your life around. They can crumble, and it feels like a betrayal, a loss of everything you held dear. But sometimes, just sometimes, that destruction is exactly what you needed, even if it feels like hell right now.

The Unraveling: Why They Can't Keep Up

Look, you're changing, and not in a small way. You're not just picking up a new hobby or getting a different haircut. You're undergoing a fundamental shift at your core. Your perceptions are changing, your values are shifting, your priorities are getting rearranged by an invisible force. You're seeing through things you used to accept, feeling things you never felt before, and often, you're experiencing a level of intensity that's just not normal for most people. And your partner? They're still back there, living in the world you used to inhabit. They're seeing the "old" you, or what they think is the "old" you, and you're not that person anymore. Or maybe you never truly were, and this energy is just stripping away the masks.

I remember feeling like I was speaking a different language. My friends, my family, my partner - they just didn't get it. I'd try to explain the energetic surges, the visions, the sudden shifts in consciousness, and I'd just get blank stares, or worse, concern that I was having a breakdown. And yeah, sometimes it feels like a breakdown. The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link) talks about this a lot - the difficulty of integrating these experiences when the world around you just doesn't have a frame of reference for them. You're seeing the matrix, and they're still watching the movie, know what I mean?

This creates a massive chasm. You're growing, expanding, often at an accelerated rate, and if your partner isn't on a similar path, or at least open to understanding it, the distance becomes unbearable. You might feel like you're stifled, held back, or misunderstood. They might feel confused, threatened, or abandoned. It's not about blame; it's just what happens when one person's reality is suddenly so different from the other's.

The Mirror Effect: Seeing What Was Always There

Kundalini isn't just about cosmic bliss, you know. It's a truth serum. It shows you everything you've been avoiding, everything you've swept under the rug. And that includes the real nature of your relationships. It shines a light on the cracks, the compromises you made, the ways you weren't being authentic, the ways they weren't being authentic with you. It's brutal. It's unflinching. And it doesn't care if it hurts.

You might suddenly see that your relationship was built on fear, or convenience, or societal expectations, rather than genuine connection. You might realize you were playing a role, trying to be someone you're not, just to keep the peace. And once you see it, you can't unsee it. That awareness is a fire. It burns away the illusion, and what's left can be stark and painful. You can't pretend anymore. You just can't. The energy won't let you. It's like your soul is screaming, "NO MORE!"

I remember looking at my own relationship and seeing all the ways I had diminished myself, all the ways I had contorted my truth to fit into someone else's idea of me. And once that insight hit, it was like a physical pain. I couldn't go back to being that person. The old patterns felt like shackles. And that's often when the relationship either has to drastically change, or it breaks. There's no middle ground when Kundalini is involved. It demands authenticity, and if the relationship can't hold that, it splinters.

Unmet Needs and Shifting Priorities

What you needed from a partner before your awakening might be completely different now. You might have craved stability, security, or a certain social status. Now, you might crave deep spiritual connection, intellectual honesty, or simply someone who can hold space for your intense experiences without judgment. Your values shift. Things that used to seem important - money, possessions, external validation - might now feel hollow or meaningless. And if your partner is still chasing those things, the disconnect becomes deep.

You might find yourself needing more solitude, more quiet, more time for inner work. You might find social gatherings overwhelming, superficial, or draining. Your partner might interpret this as you pulling away, becoming distant, or losing interest in them. And in a way, you are pulling away from the old life, the old ways of being. It's not about them; it's about you becoming more fully yourself, but it feels personal to them, doesn't it? It can feel like betrayal. And it can feel like you're being selfish. But sometimes, you have to be "selfish" to survive this process. You have to honor what your soul is demanding, even if it means disrupting everything around you.

The Great Purge: Cleansing Your Inner Circle

It's not just romantic relationships that get hit. Friendships, family dynamics - they all go under the microscope. Kundalini is a deep cleansing agent. It purges not just your inner blockages, but also anything in your external environment that doesn't serve your highest good. Sometimes, that means people. People who drain your energy, people who constantly bring you down, people who simply can't understand or accept the new you.

You might find yourself losing patience with small talk, with gossip, with superficial interactions. You might feel a strong pull to connect with people who are also exploring deeper truths, or who are at least open to it. And the people who aren't? They might just drift away, or you might find yourself actively needing to create distance. It's not always easy. It can feel incredibly lonely, especially when you're already going through such an intense internal process. But here's the truth: you can't take everyone with you on this journey. Some relationships were meant for a different chapter of your life. And this new chapter demands a new kind of support system, or at least a new level of honesty within your existing one.

I had friends I'd known for decades who just couldn't handle the "new me." I wasn't interested in the same things, I couldn't pretend to be excited about the same trivial dramas. And it hurt. It felt like I was losing a part of my identity, a part of my past. But I also realized that those friendships, while comfortable, weren't truly nourishing my soul anymore. They were keeping me small. And Kundalini doesn't want you to stay small. It wants you to expand. It wants you to become everything you're meant to be, even if that means shedding old skins, and old connections.

If you're feeling isolated, know that you're not alone. Many people who go through this experience feel like they've been dropped onto a different planet. It's why resources like When Spirit Leaps: Navigating the Process of Spiritual Awakening by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) are so important. They remind you that there's a community, even if you can't see it right now, of people who understand what you're going through. You'll find your tribe, but it might mean letting go of the old one first.

When Destruction Is Right: Reclaiming Your Truth

This is the hardest part to swallow, isn't it? That sometimes, the destruction of a relationship, no matter how painful, is actually the right thing. It's not about punishing anyone; it's about liberation. It's about clearing the path for you to become who you're meant to be, without compromise, without shrinking yourself to fit into someone else's box.

If a relationship is holding you back, if it's forcing you to deny your truth, if it's actively causing you pain or stifling your growth, then its end, while agonizing, can be a deep act of self-love. It's a declaration that you value your own spiritual journey, your own well-being, above all else. And that's not selfish; that's necessary. You can't truly serve others, or truly connect with others, if you're not connected to your own authentic self. The Kundalini is forcing that connection, whether you like it or not.

I'll tell you what, after my own relationships crumbled, after the initial shock and grief, there was a deep sense of lightness. A feeling of "Oh, this is what it feels like to be just me." No expectations, no roles to play, just raw, messy, authentic me. And that space, that emptiness, became fertile ground for something new to grow. It wasn't easy. It was terrifying. But it was also incredibly freeing. The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) really emphasizes the need for self-care and self-compassion during these periods of intense change. You're basically being reborn, and that's not a gentle process.

Rebuilding and Realigning

So yeah, you're picking up the pieces. You're probably feeling raw and vulnerable. But this isn't the end. It's a beginning. This is your chance to build relationships that are truly aligned with who you are now. Relationships based on honesty, mutual respect, and a shared understanding of the deeper mysteries of life. It might take time. You might need to spend a lot of time alone first, integrating what's happened, understanding your new self. And that's okay. That's more than okay. It's vital.

When you're ready, you'll start attracting people who connect with your new vibration. People who can meet you where you are, who don't need you to be someone you're not. They'll appreciate your depth, your intensity, your unique perspective. They'll see the light that's shining through you, not just the messy bits. And those connections, when they come, will be so much richer, so much more fulfilling than anything you had before. It's not about finding a "spiritual partner" who ticks all the boxes; it's about finding genuine connection with people who see and accept your full self.

This journey is messy, it's painful, and it often feels like you're losing everything. But what you're really losing are the things that were never truly yours to begin with, or the things that were holding you back from your true self. The kundalini is a force of liberation. It wants you free. And sometimes, freedom looks a lot like destruction for a while. Does that land?

You're strong enough for this. You wouldn't be going through it if you weren't. Breathe. Feel whatever you're feeling. And trust that on the other side of this storm, something more beautiful, more real, is waiting for you. It's not a comfortable process; it's often a "stormy search for the self," as Stanislav and Christina Grof put it in The Stormy Search for the Self (paid link). But it's your search, and it's leading you home.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
